The fact that the checking account balance is negative because of depleted liquidity buffers, is known in the literature as a signal that increases credit risk, and the model also identifies it as a factor that increases risk. [1]
The fact that there is no known savings account because of the absence of a savings account, is known in the literature as a signal that increases credit risk, but the model instead identifies it as a factor that decreases risk. [2]
The fact that there is no checking account because of the absence of a checking account, is known in the literature as a signal that decreases credit risk, but the model instead identifies it as a factor that increases risk. [3]
Overall, this applicant is likely to have a low risk of default.
